# Tank

> **Work in progress** — Tank is in early development. The protocol and
> architecture are being designed; the Haskell implementation covers core CRDT
> types, a VT100 emulator, standalone terminal mode, and a daemon skeleton.
> APIs, commands, and interfaces will change.

A protocol-centric workspace system with terminal multiplexing, per-pane
coding agents, and seamless session migration.

Named after Tank from The Matrix — the operator who loads programs, monitors
the crew, and runs the console.

## Features (MVP)

- Terminal multiplexer with pane splits and sessions
- Per-pane coding agent (Operator) with popup overlay and tool use
  - Read/write files, execute commands, grep across your codebase
  - Powered by Claude API (set `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`)
  - Toggle with `Ctrl-B a`
- Cap'n Proto binary protocol over Unix sockets
- CRDT-based state synchronization with spatial jitter buffer

## Planned Features

- Tank-to-tank federation (migrate sessions across machines)
- Directory-driven devshell activation (nix flake integration)
- Per-project process management (overmind-like)
- Ephemeral construct sandboxes
- Transport fallback: QUIC, TCP, WebSocket
- Embedded local LLM inference (llama.cpp)

## Installation

Requires [Nix](https://nixos.org/) with flakes enabled.

```bash
cd tank
nix build
# or enter dev shell:
nix develop
cabal run tank
```

## Usage

```bash
tank start          # Start the daemon
tank attach         # Attach to running daemon
tank new-cell       # Create a new terminal cell
tank list-cells     # List active cells
tank kill-server    # Stop the daemon
```

### Agent

Set `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` in your environment, then press `Ctrl-B a` inside any
pane to open the agent overlay. Type a prompt and press Enter. The agent can
read/write files, run commands, and grep your project.

## Architecture

Tank uses a hub daemon model where all components (terminal UI, coding agents,
devshell managers) are **plugs** that communicate via a Cap'n Proto binary
protocol. State is synchronized using CRDTs with an epoch-tagged LWW grid and
spatial jitter buffer for terminal screen sync.

See the [documentation site](docs-site/docs/index.md) for details:

- [Architecture](docs-site/docs/architecture.md)
- [Protocol Design](docs-site/docs/protocol.md)
- [Terminal Screen Sync](docs-site/docs/screen-sync.md)

## Influences

- [tmux](https://github.com/tmux/tmux) — terminal multiplexing model (prefix key, sessions, panes)
- [mosh](https://mosh.org/) — CRDT-based terminal state sync ([mosh paper](https://mosh.org/mosh-paper.pdf))
- [Zellij](https://github.com/zellij-org/zellij) — plugin architecture for terminal workspaces
- [overmind](https://github.com/DarthSim/overmind) — Procfile-based per-project process management
- [Cap'n Proto](https://capnproto.org/) — zero-copy binary protocol with mutable messages
- [The Matrix](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Matrix) — naming (Tank the operator, plugs, jacking in)
